name: Tourtiere #2
recipeInstructions:
1. Finely mince onion and garlic.
2. Peel and grate potatoes.
3. Place pork, onion, garlic, mace, sage, grated potato, and raisins in a large heavy pot.
4. Cover with boiling water, about 2 cups.
5. Cook, uncovered over medium heat or until meat is no longer pink and water is absorbed, 30 to 45 minutes. Stir frequently, reducing water if necessary to avoid boiling.
6. Remove from heat and set aside to cool. Skim off excess fat.
7. Preheat oven to 400°F.
8. Meanwhile, prepare pastry.
9. Line a pie plate with half of the pastry. Prick with fork and bake for 10 minutes until cooled to room temperature.
10. Pour cooled meat mixture into pie shell. Cover with top crust.
11. Crimp and seal edges and cut vents to allow steam to escape.
12. Bake for 10 minutes at 400°F, then reduce heat to 350°F and bake for 30 more minutes or until crust is light brown and filling is bubbly. Serve hot.
